title: Looking for Ganttly testers (free lifetime access)
excerpt:
Hi everyone :waving_hand: 
We’re looking for 5 testers for Ganttly - Hourly-to-monthly Gantt & Export - a monday.com app focused on Gantt timelines and exporting, built for teams that need more control than the native Ga…

posts.author: anowacka (Anna from SolDevelo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone

We’re looking for 5 testers for Ganttly - Hourly-to-monthly Gantt & Export - a monday.com app focused on Gantt timelines and exporting, built for teams that need more control than the native Gantt view.

What Ganttly does:

Gantt timelines from 15-min to monthly scale
Works with Start Date + End Date columns to define task duration
Export Gantt charts (currently to CSV, JPG, SVG)
Works with items and subitems

What we’re looking for:

  • Teams using Start Date & End Date columns instead of timeline columns for setting up the item duration
  • Anyone who needs precise control over task duration and reporting

Feedback format is flexible: short call, email, using our feedback form or sending us even a quick Loom video. We value the quality!

What you get:

  • Free lifetime access to Ganttly
  • Direct influence on the product roadmap
  • Priority support & feature discussions

If you’re interested, comment below or DM me - I’ll be happy to onboard you personally.

posts.author: schaffler33 (Amanda Schaffler)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there! I am interested in being a tester for Ganttly, if its still available. Please let me know if there is any other information you would need from me!

posts.author: david.lowe (David Lowe)
posts.cookedHtml: We are also interested. We use projects extensively and the Gratt chart is an essential tool.

posts.author: RobbenR (RobbenR)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Anne,
I’d love to see how this integration works in practice. We’re running multiple projects and programs, and our current way of working isn’t always the best fit.

It’s fine if people log in to Monday, but managers need a different kind of view. So I’d really like to play around with it and see whether it’s suitable for us.

You can expect honest feedback — the typical Dutch kind If it’s great, you’ll hear it. If it’s not… you’ll know that too.

kind regards,
Rick
